assessment_summary: The Allegro PoC is a clean demonstration of the MVP pattern in Java Swing, well-structured for its stated purpose. However, as a proof-of-concept, it carries several critical issues that would need to be resolved before production use: hardcoded URLs, complete absence of input validation, direct public field access on the View, exception swallowing, and significant code duplication between the two Swing applications.

issues.message: URL is hardcoded to 'http://localhost:8080'. Cannot be configured without source code change.
issues.solution: Externalise URL and PATH to application.properties or environment variables. Inject via constructor or dependency injection framework.

issues.message: WebSocket URI 'ws://localhost:1337/' is hardcoded and cannot be configured without recompilation.
issues.solution: Read WebSocket URI from a config file, command-line argument, or environment variable.

issues.message: All Swing component fields (textArea, name, firstName, etc.) are declared 'protected', exposing them directly to PocPresenter. This breaks View encapsulation.
issues.solution: Declare fields private and expose them via public getter methods or a dedicated ViewData / ViewModel object.

issues.message: The 'model' EnumMap field is declared public, allowing any class to directly mutate model state without going through the designated API.
issues.solution: Declare 'model' private. Provide specific getter/setter methods or a typed accessor for each property.

issues.message: IOException and InterruptedException from model.action() are caught and re-thrown as RuntimeException with no user notification. This will crash the Swing Event Dispatch Thread silently.
issues.solution: Catch exceptions, show a JOptionPane error dialog to the user, and log the stack trace. For InterruptedException, also call Thread.currentThread().interrupt().

issues.message: model.get(val).getField() may return null for any uninitialised field. Calling .toString() on a null will throw NullPointerException at runtime.
issues.solution: Add null checks before calling toString(), or initialise fields with empty-string defaults rather than null. Use Optional<T> or provide a safe serialisation helper.

issues.message: HttpURLConnection is not closed in a finally block or try-with-resources. If an exception occurs before disconnect(), the connection will leak.
issues.solution: Wrap the connection lifecycle in a try-finally block ensuring connection.disconnect() is always called, or migrate to java.net.http.HttpClient (Java 11+).

issues.message: Uses legacy java.net.HttpURLConnection API. Java 11+ includes the modern java.net.http.HttpClient with async support, better resource management, and cleaner API.
issues.solution: Replace HttpURLConnection with HttpClient.newHttpClient() / HttpRequest / HttpResponse for modern idiomatic Java.

issues.message: model.action() is called on the Swing EDT (Event Dispatch Thread) when the button is clicked. This blocks the UI thread during the synchronous HTTP call, freezing the window.
issues.solution: Execute model.action() on a background thread using SwingWorker or CompletableFuture. Update the UI in the done()/EDT callback.

issues.message: ViewData class is completely empty. It is not referenced or used anywhere in the codebase.
issues.solution: Either implement the ViewData class as a DTO for passing form state, or delete it.

issues.message: The JSON parsing in extract() and toSearchResult() uses boolean flag variables (e.g., boolean name = false; boolean first = false;) instead of a state machine or dedicated JSON library. This is fragile, verbose, and difficult to maintain.
issues.solution: Use a JSON object-mapping library (Jackson, Gson) or at minimum refactor to use JsonObject via javax.json.JsonReader for cleaner field access.

issues.message: textArea is added to the panel twice: once without constraints (line 188: panel.add(textArea)) and once with GridBagConstraints (line 189: panel.add(textArea, c)). In Swing, a component can only have one parent; the second add() removes it from the first position.
issues.solution: Remove the duplicate panel.add(textArea) call on line 188 with no constraints.

issues.message: Same duplicate textArea add() bug as PocView.java — textArea is added to panel twice (lines 221-222).
issues.solution: Remove the duplicate panel.add(textArea) call without constraints.

issues.message: HttpBinService is instantiated directly inside PocModel with 'new HttpBinService()'. This creates tight coupling and makes PocModel untestable without the real HTTP service.
issues.solution: Inject HttpBinService through the constructor. Define an interface (e.g., ISubmissionService) to allow mocking in tests.

technical_debt.description: Massive code duplication: the UI layout code in websocket/Main.initUI() is ~95% identical to com/poc/presentation/PocView.initUI(). Also, the WebSocket version has a separate static field for every UI component instead of encapsulated fields.
technical_debt.suggested_fix: Extract the shared UI layout into a reusable AllegroFormPanel class. The WebSocket Main should use PocView or the shared component.

technical_debt.description: The WebSocket client (websocket/Main.java) is a monolithic god-class containing UI layout, WebSocket lifecycle, JSON parsing, data model (SearchResult), and application logic all in one file. It does not follow the MVP pattern used by the other module.
technical_debt.suggested_fix: Refactor WebSocket client to follow the same MVP structure: extract a WebSocketView, WebSocketModel (managing session state), and WebSocketPresenter.

technical_debt.description: Zero test coverage across the entire project. No unit tests, integration tests, or UI tests exist.
technical_debt.suggested_fix: Add JUnit 5 unit tests for PocModel, HttpBinService, ValueModel, EventEmitter, and the JSON parsing logic in websocket/Main.java. Use Mockito to mock HttpBinService.

technical_debt.description: No Javadoc on any class or method except the @OnOpen/@OnClose/@OnMessage annotations in websocket/Main.java (which are package-level Javadoc, not custom). Internal logic has minimal comments.
technical_debt.suggested_fix: Add class-level and method-level Javadoc to all public classes and methods.

technical_debt.description: EventEmitter uses a simple synchronous ArrayList of listeners with no thread safety. Concurrent subscribe() and emit() calls (e.g., from background threads) would cause ConcurrentModificationException.
technical_debt.suggested_fix: Use CopyOnWriteArrayList<EventListener> for thread-safe iteration, or use PropertyChangeSupport.

technical_debt.description: Debug console output (System.out.println) is scattered throughout PocPresenter, PocModel, and HttpBinService. These are not appropriate for production use.
technical_debt.suggested_fix: Replace System.out.println with a proper logging framework (SLF4J + Logback or java.util.logging).
